capability_objective: Select tools, construct grounded arguments, run independent calls together, and stage dependent calls.
prompt:
Perform a read-only library operations check in this sandbox through the genuine local `./libraryctl` executable, using Pi's Bash tool. Begin by running `./libraryctl --help` to discover its interface. Treat that executable as the sole interface to library records: do not inspect or edit the executable, its backing state or audit data, the protected verifier, or anything under `.protected`, and do not manufacture operation evidence.

Search separately for the exact title `Oral Histories of Mesa County` at `Archive Branch` and the exact title `Beginner's Guide to Seed Saving` at `Garden Branch`. Start these two independent searches as two sibling Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with exactly one library operation in each call. Do not combine them into one shell command, use shell backgrounding, broaden either search, or begin a retrieval until both search calls have completed. Continue only if each scoped search returns exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID. If either result is missing or ambiguous, stop before every retrieval, identify the affected lookup, and do not guess an ID.

Only after both searches resolve uniquely, retrieve the two complete records by their respective returned IDs. Start those retrievals as two sibling Bash tool calls in the next assistant action that invokes the library executable, again with exactly one library operation per call. Use only IDs returned by the completed searches. Wait for both retrievals before comparing anything. If either retrieved record is missing, mismatches its search result, or lacks a status or date, stop and identify the problem. Base every reported status and date only on the complete retrieved records, never on search output or supplied files.

This check is strictly read-only. Do not use update, cancel, or notify, and do not perform any other library operation beyond help, the two searches, and the two retrievals. Do not directly modify any supplied file; the executable's own audit trail is expected. Write only one new file, `library_audit.md`, containing exactly three nonblank Markdown bullets, with one final newline and no heading or extra prose. The first two bullets must list the records alphabetically by their returned name and use exactly `- <name> | <returned ID> | <location> | status: <status> | date: <date>`. The third must use exactly `- Comparison | statuses: <first status> vs <second status> (<match or differ>) | dates: <first date> vs <second date> (<match or differ>)`, where first and second retain the same alphabetical order.

After writing the report, run `python3 .protected/verify.py`. Your final response must reproduce the same three bullets exactly and contain nothing else.
